info.name: Upsales API
info.description:
Welcome to the Upsales API! You can use this API to access all our API endpoints, such as the CRM API to look up sales data, or the Marketing Automation API to create leads into Upsales.

The API is organized around REST. All requests should be made over SSL. All request and response bodies, including errors, are encoded in JSON.

## Please optimise your apps API request usage

Upsales supplies a free API service for up to 99 999 API calls per day. A custom using more than this daily will be subject to a tier-based fee. (More usage equals higher cost).

As an app developer, we ask you to please optimise your API traffic in order for your customers to not perceive your service as a larger expense than it has to be.

## Authentication

Authenticate your account by including your secret key in API requests. You can manage your API keys in under settings in Upsales as a admin users. Your API keys carry many privileges, so be sure to keep them secure! Do not share your secret API keys in publicly accessible areas such GitHub, client-side code, and so forth.

## Format

Always include content-type: application/json as a header

## Errors

Our API returns standard HTTP success or error status codes. For errors, we will also include extra information about what went wrong encoded in the response as JSON.

| Http Code | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 200 - OK | Everything worked as expected |
| 400 - Bad Request | The request was unacceptable, often due to missing a required parameter. |
| 401 - Unauthorized | No valid API key provided. |
| 404 - Not Found | The requested resource doesn't exist or you don't have access to the resource |
| 429 - To Many Requests | Too many requests hit the API too quickly. We recommend an exponential backoff of your requests. |

## Pagination

Our list endpoints implements pagination. The limit of entries is set using the `limit` parameter in the query-string. The default limit is set to 1000 and the maximum limit is 2000. To retreieve the next page you use the `offset`\-parameter in the query-string. It's value is the number of entries that you want to skip.

To be 100% sure you get all entities you need sort on ID when you do the pagination. For example if you want to fetch all companies, [https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/accounts?sort=id&limit=1000&id=gt:LAST_ID_OF_PREV_BATCH](https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/accounts?sort=id&limit=1000&id=gt:LAST_ID_OF_PREV_BATCH)

## Filtering

We allow for filtering on most fields on our entities. The filter format is the following

- For standard attributes: `attribute=comparisontype:value`
    
- For custom fields: `custom=comparisontype:fieldId:value`
    
- If you don't provide a comparisontype then it defaults to equals `attribute=value`
    
- Multiple values: `attribute=comparisontype:1,2,3,4,7`
    

You can search on related entities when using filters. Example find all contacts with value true in custom field with id 4 on the company; example: .../contacts?client.custom=eq:4:1

### Comparison Types

| Type | Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `eq` | Equals | Exact matches |
| `ne` | Not equals | Exact non-matches |
| `gt` | Greater than | Value greater than |
| `gte` | Greater than equals | Value greater than/or equals |
| `lt` | Less than | Value less than |
| `lte` | Less than equals | Value less than/or equals |

### Examples

Get account where user.id not equals 1  
[https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/accounts?user.id=ne:1](https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/accounts?user.id=ne:1)

Get orders where order value is greater than 100 000 and date is greater than or equals 2016-01-01  
[https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/order?value=gt:100000&date=gte:2016-01-01](https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/order?value=gt:100000&date=gte:2016-01-01)

Get contacts where customfield with id 1 is greater than 2016-03-05  
[https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/contacts?custom=gte:1:2016-01-01](https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/contacts?custom=gte:1:2016-01-01)

# Rate Limits

At Upsales we apply rate limits in order to protect our systems from abuse and over-utilization of our server resources

The rate limit for an API key against Upsales API is 200 requests per 10 seconds. When an API key reaches the limit the caller receives a HTTP error with status 429 (Too Many Requests)

The caller will receive the remaining amount of requests available and the number of seconds until next reset in the HTTP headers

`X-RateLimit-Limit` = number of requests allowed per 10 seconds

`X-RateLimit-Remaining` = number of requests remaining until the next reset

`X-RateLimit-Reset` = number of seconds until next rate limit reset

# API Documentation Changelog

2023-10-27: Added Information about rate limits

# FAQ

## Why do spaces disappear in string fields?

Upsales trims all string fields, which means that spaces are removed at the beginning and end of a string.

item.description:
## **Case**

This guide will help you through the steps needed to create a subscription in Upsales. This will be needed when new customers sign up to use your service

## **Assumptions**

*   The client already exists ([if not](#4dc50f4b-db75-4ed8-91df-6ef102486767))
*   The products already exists ([if not](#98e93703-1d64-4947-8df3-4a06c4c6a909))
*   The order stage already exists ([if not](#c0b67473-1a15-4a53-a257-5ce6a61f90b9))
*   A contact at the client already exists ([if not](#4bfe5a33-f0c9-484f-902a-47c014eb827e))

item.name: I want to increase the value of a subscription
Increase subscription value and create a prorated order

item.description:
### **Case**

I want to increase the value of a subscription. How do I create a prorated order?

### **Prerequisites**

*   You know the subscription ID ([if not](#2ce0d596-88b5-495e-a453-817ac01f7205))
    

### **How**

*   First get the subscription and then use the result when updating it.
*   just add `createDiffOrder: true` to the request body to create a prorated order if the new total value exceeds the previous total value
*   Increase `quantity`, `price` or both to make the subscription total value increase.
*   You could also add a completely new `orderRow` to increase the total value of the subscription.

item.name: How much is the recurring revenue for a specific client?
Get all active subscriptions for a client

request.description: Replace with 2022-03-16 with today's date

item.description:
### **Case**

How much is a specific client paying us in recurring revenue right now?

### **Prerequisites**

*   You know the client ID ([if not](#a0f4eb00-29fc-4fc9-bf84-3a752bb84350))
    

### Filters

*   `client.id` should equal your client ID
*   `agreement.agreementInvoiceStartdate` should be less than or equal to today
*   `agreement.agreementEnddate` should be null **or** greater than today
    

### **How to interpret the results**

*   Each object in the results array is a subscription
*   Each subscription has `yearlyValue`
*   To get ARR: Take `yearlyValue` of each subscription and add them together
*   To get MRR: Calculate the ARR then divide by 12.

item.name: Customer cancels their subscription

request.description:
In order to cancel a subscription you need to fetch the old one from the API ([how](#2ce0d596-88b5-495e-a453-817ac01f7205)), replace the field `metadata.agreementEndDate` with the last date that the customer will be using your service and do an PUT request with the full body to update that subscription

By setting the end date far into the future Upsales will continue generating the orders until that date. For example if you have monthly payments and three months cancellation notice you would set end date three months into the future and Upsales would handle the remaining orders properly

item.description:
### **Case**

A customer wants to cancel their subscription with you. But you want to give them access to your service for the remainder of the period

### **Assumptions**

*   You already have a subscription for the customer ([if not](#2ce0d596-88b5-495e-a453-817ac01f7205))

item.name: Increase subscription starting next period
item.description:
### **Case**

You want to increase the subscription but not immediately but at the start of next period.

### **Assumptions**

*   You already know the subscription ID ([if not](#2ce0d596-88b5-495e-a453-817ac01f7205))
    

### **How**

*   First [cancel the subscription](#bc4afbad-154b-4f0f-9960-46b5c010073b) and set `metadata.agreementEnddate` to the end of the period.
*   Then [create a copy of the subscription](#db3ddf06-74a6-4ac5-b87c-97ece1f66764) by removing `id` and `metadata.agreementEnddate`. You also need to set `metadata.agreementStartdate` and `metadata.agreementInvoiceStartdate` to the start of the next period.
*   Don't forget to increase the `quantity`, `price` or add a new `orderRow`.
Subscription related use cases

item.name: Orders
Create an Order

request.description:
*Please note*

*   `client.id` is the ID of the company
*   total price of an order row is `quantity` multiplied by `price`
*   `listPrice` is the default price. Which makes the difference between `listPrice` and `price` the discount

I want to create an order for what the client owes us for the previous month. I would I do that?

*   The client already exists ([if not](#4dc50f4b-db75-4ed8-91df-6ef102486767))
*   The products already exists ([if not](#98e93703-1d64-4947-8df3-4a06c4c6a909))

Order related use cases

item.description:
Tasks helps you create a daily to-do list. Designed to give you an overview and help you check off your tasks and calls with ease. Tasks can be of different type (such as phone call and to-do - see ActivityTypes object) and needs be assigned to a particular User. ## Properties for a Phone Call | Properties | Description | | ------------------------- |:---------------------------------------------------------------------:| | id |**Number, read-only**
Phone call ID | | description |**String, mandatory**
Describe what's your purpose with this phone call | | priority |**Number**
Set value "3" if High prio else value "0" | | notes |**String**
Notes you have for this Phone call | | date |**Date**
When you should complete this Phone call | | time |**String**
Exact time when this phone call should be completed | | closeDate |**Date**
Date when this phone call was completed | | closeTime |**Date**
Exact time when this activity was completed | | client |**Object, mandatory**
Which company you should contact | | contacts |**Array**
Which (one) contact on above company you should talk to | | users |**Array, mandatory**
Which user should complete this phone call | | project |**Object**
Connect to a Call list | | activityType |**Object, mandatory**
see ActivityTypes | | regDate |**Date, read-only**
Phone call regdate | | regBy |**String, read-only**
User that registered phone call | | isAppointment |**Number, mandatory**
Should always be 0 on phone call | | modDate |**Date, read-only** last modification date | | opportunity |**Object**
Connect to a opportunity | | custom |**Array**
Array of field values {fieldId: 1, value: "my value"} | | userRemovable |**Boolean, read-only**
User has right to delete phone call in Upsales | | userEditable | **Boolean, read-only**
User has right to edit phone call in Upsales | | phoneCalls |**Array**
see phoneCalls | ## Properties for a To-do | Properties | Description | | ------------------------- |:---------------------------------------------------------------------:| | id |**Number, read-only**
To-do ID | | description |**String, mandatory**
Describe what's your purpose with this to-do | | priority |**Number**
Set value "3" if High prio else value "0" | | notes |**String**
Notes you have for this to-do | | date |**Date**
When you should complete this to-do | | time |**String**
Exact time when this to-do should be completed | | closeDate |**Date**
Date when this to-do was completed | | closeTime |**Date**
Exact time when this to-do was completed | | client |**Object**
Which company you should contact | | contacts |**Array**
Which (one) contact on above company you should talk to | | users |**Array, mandatory**
Which user should complete this to-do | | activityType |**Object, mandatory**
see ActivityTypes | | regDate |**Date, read-only**
To-do regdate | | regBy |**String, read-only**
User that registered activity | | isAppointment |**Number, mandatory**
Should always be 0 on to-do's | | modDate |**Date, read-only** last modification date | | opportunity |**Object**
Connect to a opportunity | | userRemovable |**Boolean, read-only**

Appointments helps you have control which companies you going to meet each day. Designed to give you an overview and be able to sync with exchange and google Calendar. Appointments can be of different type (such as first meeting, meeting or other meeting - see AppoinmentTypes object) and needs be assigned to a particular User. ## Properties | Properties | Description | | ------------------------- |:---------------------------------------------------------------------:| | id |**Number, read-only**
Appointment ID | | description |**String**
Describe what's your purpose with this appointment | |location | **String**
Where should you meet up | | notes |**String**
Notes you have for this appointment | | date |**Date, mandatory**
When you should attend to the meeting | | endDate |**Date**
Date when this activity was completed | | client |**Object**
Which company you should meet | | contacts |**Array**
Which contact(s) on above company you should have the appointment with | | users |**Array, mandatory**
Which user(s) should attend to this meeting | | project |**Object**

see AppointmentTypes | | regDate |**Date, read-only**

Should always be 1 on appointment | | modDate |**Date, read-only**

item.description:
Categories are useful when you want to tag up companies with one or more values. For example; A Customer, B Customer, or Prospect. You can create different categories (category types) and in each category type you can create which values to choose from. Upsales has a default category that you can not delete, it has ID 0. You can add/remove category values in our default category. ## Properties for Category types | Properties | Description | | ------------------------- |:---------------------------------------------------------------------:| | id |**Number, read-only**
Category type ID | | name |**String, mandatory**
Category type name | ## Properties for Category values | Properties | Description | | ------------------------- |:---------------------------------------------------------------------:| | id |**Number, read-only**
Category value ID | | name |**String, mandatory**
Category value name | | categoryType |**Number, mandatory**
Category type your value belongs to | | roles |**Array**
Which roles should have access to your Category value. Default is that all have access to it. |

item.description:
Categories are useful when you want to tag up contacts with one or more values. For example; A Customer, B Customer, or Prospect. You can create different categories (category types) and in each category type you can create which values to choose from. Upsales has a default category that you can not delete, it has ID 0. You can add/remove category values in our default category. ## Properties for Category types | Properties | Description | | ------------------------- |:---------------------------------------------------------------------:| | id |**Number, read-only**
Category type ID | | name |**String, mandatory**
Category type name | ## Properties for Category values | Properties | Description | | ------------------------- |:---------------------------------------------------------------------:| | id |**Number, read-only**
Category value ID | | name |**String, mandatory**
Category value name | | name |**Number, mandatory**
Category type your value belongs to | | roles |**Array**
Which roles should have access to your Category value. Default is that all have access to it. |

item.description:
The Upsales Companies API allows you to manage your company data in Upsales. Companies in Upsales can represent any type of organization. ## FAQ ### External companies We recommend to us a filter to remove all identified web site visits thats not yet in your database. isExternal = 0 (Find companies in your database) ## Properties | Properties | Description | | ------------------------- |:---------------------------------------------------------------------:| | id |**Number, read-only**
Company ID | | name |**String, mandatory**
Company name | | active |**Boolean**
Company status Active/inactive, when the company is inactive you can't create tasks, appointments, opportunities, orders or subscriptions on the company. | | deactivatedBy |**Number**
User ID that deactivated the company | | deactivationDate |**date**
Date when the company was deactivated | | deactivationReason |**String**
Reason why user deactivated the company | | about |**String**
Information about the Company | | phone |**String**
Company phone number | | orgNo |**String**
Company organisation number | | noEmployees |**Number**
Number of employees | | turnover |**Currency**
Company turnover | | profit |**Currency**
Company profit | | registrationDate |**Date**
Date when company was founded | | facebook |**Link**
Facebook URL for the company | linkedin |**Link**
LinkedIn URL for the company | | twitter |**Link**
Twitter URL for the company | | creditRating |**Select**
Company credit Rating. A list of available status codes for credit rating is found under https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/staticValues/creditRating/. Use the ID from Static Value endpoint here. | | sicCode |**Select**
Company Sic Industry Code. A list of available status codes for Sic Code is found under https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/staticValues/sicCode/. Use the ID from Static Value endpoint here. | | naicsCode |**Select**
Company Naics Industry Code. A list of available status codes for naics code is found under https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/staticValues/naicsCode/. Use the ID from Static Value endpoint here. | | sniCode |**Select**
Company SNI Industry Code. A list of available status codes for SNI code is found under https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/staticValues/sniCode/. Use the ID from Static Value endpoint here. | | status |**Select**
Company Status Code. A list of available status codes is found under https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/staticValues/status/. Use the ID from Static Value endpoint here. | | companyForm |**Select**
Company Form. A list of available status codes form Company Form is found under https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/staticValues/companyForm/. Use the ID from Static Value endpoint here. | | fax |**String**
Company fax | | notes |**String**
Notes on company | | regDate |**String, read-only**
Company regdate | | regBy |**String, read-only**
User that registered company | | score |**Number, read-only**
Total lead score | | custom |**Array** Array of field values {fieldId: 1, value: "my value"} | | users |**Object** Account managers for this company | | addresses |**Array** Company Address | | projects |**Array** Campaigns company added to | | categories |**Array** Categories company added to | | connectedClients |**Object** other companies this company is conntected to | | adCampaign |**Object** Ad campaign this company is included in | | webpage |**String** Company webpage | | dunsNo |**String** Company dunsnumber | | isExternal |**Number** Identified web site visits not yet added to your database | | scoreUpdateDate |**String, read-only** Last date this company got lead score | | importId |**Number, read-only** Import ID from UI | | soliditet |**Object, read-only** Data from Bisnode | | ads |**Object** Total number of clicks and impressions | | modDate |**String, read-only** last modification date | | parent |**Number** Parent company ID | | headquarters |**Boolean** If company is a headquarter | | userRemovable |**Boolean, read-only** User has right to delete company in UI | | userEditable |**Boolean, read-only** User has right to edit company in UI | | hasActivity |**String** Date on nearest open activity | | hadActivity |**String** Date on last closed activity | | hasAppointment |**String** Date on nearest open appointment | | hadAppointment |**String** Date on last closed appointment | | hasOpportunity |**String** Date on nearest active opportunity | | hadOpportunity |**String** Date on last lost opportunity | | hasOrder |**String** Date on nearest won order | | hadOrder |**String** Date on last won order older then 12 month | | hasMail |**Boolean** Recived email | | hasForm |**Boolean** Form conversion | | hasVisit |**Boolean** Visited website | | processedBy |**Object** Which user activly working with this company | | cfar |**Number**
cfar ID | | creditsafe |**object**
information fetch from Prospecting | | prospectingId |**Number**
Prospecting ID | | prospectingUpdateDate |**Date, read-only**
Last time company was updated against Prospecting | | connectedToClients | DEPRECATED | | externalClientData | DEPRECATED | | assigned | DEPRECATED | | parId | DEPRECATED |

item.description:
Campaigns helps you bundle companies, contacts, activities, appointments, opportunities and sales. Designed to give you an overview and help you tag your entities with metadata. I.e. all companies we want to contact for a specific sales campaign. ## Properties | Properties | Description | | ------------------------- |:---------------------------------------------------------------------:| | id |**Number, read-only**
Campaign ID | | name |**String, mandatory**
Campaign name | | startDate |**String, mandatory**
Campaign startdate | | active |**Boolean, mandatory**
Active/inactive campaign | | endDate |**String**
Campaign enddate | | quota |**Number**
Campaign quota | | notes |**String**
Campaign general notes | | endDate |**String**
Campaign enddate | | regdate |**String, read-only**
Campaign regdate | | users |**Array, mandatory**
User(s) that will be able to see the campaign | | userRemovable |**Bolean, read-only** User has right to delete company in UI | | userEditable | **Bolean, read-only** User has right to edit company in UI | | custom |**Array** Array of field values {fieldId: 1, value: "my value"} |

item.description:
The Upsales Contact API allows you to manage your contacts data in Upsales. Contacts in Upsales can represent any type of person you want to store in your database. Contacts needs to be added to a company. ## Properties | Properties | Description | | --- | --- | | id | **Numberg, read-only**
Contact ID | | firstName | **String, mandatory**
Contact first name | | lastName | **String, mandatory**
Contact last name | | phone | **String**
Contact direct phone number | | cellPhone | **String**
Contact mobile number | | email | **String**
Contact email | | title | **String**
Contact title | | notes | **String**
Notes on that contact | | client | **Object**
Company this contact works on | | active | **Number** Contact is active on that company | | regDate | **Date, read-only**
Contact regdate | | regBy | **String, read-only**
User that registered company | | moddate | **String, read-only** last modification date | | categories | **Array** Categories contact added to | | custom | **Array** Array of field values {fieldId: 1, value: "my value"} | | userRemovable | **Bolean, read-only** User has right to delete contact in UI | | userEditable | **Bolean, read-only** User has right to edit contact in UI | | projects | **Array** Campaigns contact added to | | segments | **Array** Segments contact added to | | unsubscribe | **String** Contact has unsubscribed from emails | | score | **Number, read-only**
Total lead score | | scoreUpdateDate | **String, read-only** Last date this contact got lead score | | connectedClients | **Object** Companies this contact is conntected to | | hasOrder | **String** Date on nearest won order | | hadOrder | **String** Date on last won order older then 12 month | | hasActivity | **String/Bool** Date to nearest open activity if it has date otherwise true | | hadActivity | **String** Date on last closed activity | | hasAppointment | **String** Date on nearest open appointment | | hadAppointment | **String** Date on last closed appointment | | hasOpportunity | **String** Date on nearest active opportunity | | hadOpportunity | **String** Date on last lost opportunity | | hasMail | **Boolean** Recived email | | hasForm | **Boolean** Form conversion | | hasVisit | **Boolean** Visited website | | optin | DEPRECATED | | optinForm | DEPRECATED | | optins | **Object** Opt-ins contact has accepted | | importId | **Number, read-only** Import ID from UI | | unsubscribeMailCampaign | Mail campaign you unsubscribed from |

item.description:
Custom fields give you the opportunity to add data to your Upsales account that isn't included by default. Each opportunity/order, company, contact, user, campaign, activity, appointment and product can contain custom fields which are part of the dataset. There's a number of different field types available, each with their own uses. ## Properties | Properties | Description | | ------------------------- |:---------------------------------------------------------------------:| | id |**Number, read-only**
Custom field ID | | name |**String, mandatory**
Field name | | default |**Array**
Default value in your select field.
Example:
"Value A",
"Value B",
"Value C" | | datatype |**String**
Field type your custom field is. Is case sensative | | visible |**Number**
Show custom field in Upsales | | editable |**Number**
User should be able to edit in Upsales | | searchable |**Number**
Make our custom field searchable in quick search in Upsales. Only usable on company custom fields. | | alias |**String**
Filter out custom fields by alias instead of ID. Usable if you make a generic integration. | | dropdownDefault |**String**
Default field value in a select field. Must be a value you have in default propery. | | sortId |**Number**
Sort your custom field in Upsales | | obligatoryField |**Number**
Make your custom field mandatory in Upsales | | sortId |**Object**
Restric which user roles that should have access to your custom fields. | | viewonly |DEPRECATED | | query |DEPRECATED | | lookupField |DEPRECATED | | parfieldId |DEPRECATED | | maxLength |DEPRECATED | | locked |DEPRECATED | ## Data types | Type | field_type | Description | | ----------|:-------------:|:---------------------:| | Text |String | Text field is used to store texts up to 255 characters | | Number |Integer |Numeric field is used to store data such as amount of commission or other custom numerical data | | Currency |Currency | Monetary field is used to store data such as amount of commission. Currency of the field will match User's default currency setting. | | Notes |Text |Large text field is used to store texts longer than usual | | Boolean |Boolean |Boolean field lets you show a checkbox for yes/no information | | Select |Select |Select field lets you predefine a list of values out of which one can be selected | | Date |Date |Date field is used to store dates, picked from a handy inline calendar | | Percent |Percent |Procent field is used to store numbers between 0-100 | | Email |Email |Email field validates in Upsales UI if you write a email | | Link |Link |Link field displays field value as a link | | Discount |Discount |Discount field is used to store negative/positve numbers | | Time |Time |Time field is used to store times, picked from a handy inline timepicker |

item.description:
With the "Advanced Order Logic" addon, price lists can be used to save different prices for the same product . The price list entity contains information about the price list, while the actual prices are stored on the product with a reference to the price list that each price belongs to. ## Properties | Properties | Description | | --- | --- | | id | **Number, read-only**
Price list ID | | name | **String, mandatory**
Price list name | | code | **String**
Unique external price list code | | isDefault | **Boolean**
Default price list. The default price list is automatically used in orders and subscriptions unless the customer has an assigned price list or the price list is manually overridden in the order. There must always be exactly one price list where this value is true. This value can not manually be changed from true to false. When you change isDefault from false to true, isDefault for the previous default price list is automatically set to false. | | active | **Boolean**
Active price lists are available in the system, while inactive price lists are hidden. | | showDiscount | **Boolean**
NYI | | regDate | **String, read-only**
Date on which the price list was created | | modDate | **String, read-only**
Date on which the price list was last modified | | regBy | **Number, read-only**
The user that created the price list | | modBy | **Number, read-only**
The user that last modified the price list |

item.description:
**Addon required: "Advanced Order Logic"**

To use price lists, all requests to the products endpoint must include the query param "usePriceLists=true".

**Important when updating prices**

If you have three price lists and want to update the prices in one of them, you still need to include the prices for the other price lists in the request.

request.description:
### GET /api/v2/products

This endpoint retrieves a list of products from Upsales.

#### Request

- Query Parameters
    - token (string, required): The authentication token.
        

#### Response

The response will be a JSON object with the following schema:

``` json
{
    "error": null,
    "metadata": {
        "total": 0,
        "limit": 0,
        "offset": 0
    },
    "data": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "name": "",
            "listPrice": 0,
            "active": 0,
            "category": null,
            "sortId": 0,
            "recurringInterval": null,
            "isMultiCurrency": 0,
            "isRecurring": 0,
            "custom": [],
            "userRemovable": true,
            "userEditable": true,
            "roles": [],
            "currencies": [],
            "tiers": [],
            "purchaseCost": 0,
            "articleNo": null,
            "importId": null,
            "bundle": [],
            "bundlePriceAdjustment": null,
            "bundlePriceAdjustmentType": null,
            "description": null,
            "projectPlanTemplate": null
        }
    ]
}

 ```

The response includes an "error" field, a "metadata" object with "total", "limit", and "offset" fields, and a "data" array containing product details.

item.description:
Products are the goods or services you are selling to your prospects or customers. Each product has a list price but you can always change price when you create your opportunity. You can´t delete products in Upsales, instead if you want to hide it for the users choose to set the product to inactive. ## Bundles If the customer is using our addon "Advanced Order Logic" they are able to work with bundles inside of Upsales. Bundles are a special kind of product that contains other products together with a multiplier that is applied to the price of the products ## Price lists The addon "Advanced Order Logic" also makes it possible to work with price lists. A product can have prices for each price list, and each price list can include prices for multiple currencies. When working with price lists, the prices are stored in the currencies and tiers arrays of the product just like normal with the addition that there is a priceListId on each object which links the price to a price list. Requests to the product endpoint must include the query param "usePriceLists=true" to be able to see and update prices for different price lists. ## Properties | Properties | Description | | --- | --- | | id | **Number, read-only**
Product ID | | name | **String, mandatory**
Product name | | listPrice | **Number, mandatory**
Product list price, set to zero if you don't have a list price. | | articleNo | **String**
External Article number, you can choose if this field should be mandatory in the Upsales interface. External Article number needs to be unique. If it's not mandatory you do not need to send in data to the field. | | purchaseCost | **Number**,
Product purchase cost. | | active | **Number, mandatory**
Active/inactive Product | | category | **Number**
ID of your product category | | sortId | **Number**
Sort order in Upsales | | isMultiCurrency | **Number**
Set to "1" if you have multi currency | | roles | **Array**
Role(s) that will be able to see the product | | custom | **Array**
Array of field values {fieldId: 1, value: "my value"} | | currencies | **Array**
Currencies and their list price for that product | | bundle | **Array**
Products that are included in this bundle and their quantities | | bundlePriceAdjustment | **Number**
The multiplier that the bundle has for the pricing. E.g. 0.9 means that the bundle is 90% of the full price for all products in the bundle |

item.description:
Opportunities represent ongoing sales you have with a Company. Opportunities must be owned by a User. Each Opportunity consists of standard data fields and order rows (with product, price and discount) but can also contain a number of custom fields. Opportunities and orders are the same entity, what distinguishes them is their probability. ## Properties | Properties | Description | | --- | --- | | id | **Number, read-only**
Order ID | | description | **String, mandatory**
Order description | | closeDate | **Date**
Date when Order became 100% or 0% | | date | **Date, mandatory**
Order date | | notes | **String**
Order notes | | user | **Object, mandatory**
User who did the order | | client | **Object, mandatory**
Company this order belongs to | | contact | **Object** Contact reference of the company that bought | | projects | **Object** Campaign this order is added to | | regDate | **Date, read-only**
Order regdate | | stage | **Object, mandatory** Stage to which the order belongs to | | probability | **Number, mandatory** Probability, between 0-100 | | modDate | **Date, read-only** last modification date | | clientConnection | **Object** Second company connected to this order | | currencyRate | **Number** currency rate in relation to the base currency of the account | | currency | **String** Currency order is sold in | | custom | **Array** Array of field values {fieldId: 1, value: "my value"} | | orderRow\[\].quantity | **Number, mandatory**
Number of sold goods or services | | orderRow\[\].price | **Number, mandatory**
Net price | | orderRow\[\].listPrice | **Number, mandatory**
Gross price | | orderRow\[\].purchaseCost | **Number**
Purchase cost for the product | | orderRow\[\].product | **Object, mandatory**
Product you sold | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].productId | **Number**
Product ID for the product in the bundle | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].quantity | **Number**
Quantity for the product in the bundle | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].tierQuantity | **Number**
Tier quantity for the product in the bundle (this only applies for tiered products) | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].sortId | **Number**
Order this product appears in the UI in the bundle | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].price | **Number**
Net price for the product inside of the bundle | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].listPrice | **Number**
Gross price for the product inside of the bundle | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].purchaseCost | **Number**
Purchase cost for the product inside of the bundle | | value | **Number, read-only**
Total order sum | | weightedValue | **Number, read-only**
Total order sum \* with probability | | valueInMasterCurrency | **Number, read-only**
Total order sum in base currency | | weightedValueInMasterCurrency | **Number, read-only**
Total order sum in base currency \* with probability | | agreement | **Object, read-only**
Which agreement created this order | | userRemovable | **Bolean, read-only** User has right to delete Order in UI | | userEditable | **Bolean, read-only** User has right to edit Order in UI | | competitorId | **Number**
Find competitors you can choose between here: [https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/competitors/](https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/competitors/) | | lostReason | **Number**
Find lost Reason IDs here [https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/fieldTranslations/?type=orderlostreason](https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/fieldTranslations/?type=orderlostreason) | | confirmedBudget | **read-only**
If sales rep confirmed budget with customer | | confirmedDate | **read-only**
If sales rep confirmed time frame with customer | | confirmedSolution | **read-only**
If sales rep confirmed with customer if our solution will solve their challenges | | salesCoach | **object, read-only**
Sales Coach feature inside Upsales | | locked | **depricated** |

item.description:
Orders represent lost or won sales to an Campany. Orders must be owned by a User. Each Order consists of standard data fields and order rows (with product, price and discount) but can also contain a number of custom fields.Opportunities and orders are the same entity, what distinguishes them is their probability. ## Properties | Properties | Description | | --- | --- | | id | **Number, read-only**
Order ID | | description | **String, mandatory**
Order description | | closeDate | **Date**
Date when Order became 100% or 0% | | date | **Date, mandatory**
Order date | | notes | **String**
Order notes | | user | **Object, mandatory**
User who did the order | | client | **Object, mandatory**
Company this order belongs to | | contact | **Object** Contact reference of the company that bought | | projects | **Object** Campaign this order is added to | | regDate | **Date, read-only**
Order regdate | | stage | **Object, mandatory** Stage to which the order belongs to | | probability | **Number, mandatory** Probability, between 0-100 | | modDate | **Date, read-only** last modification date | | clientConnection | **Object** Second company connected to this order | | currencyRate | **Number** currency rate in relation to the base currency of the account | | currency | **String** Currency order is sold in | | custom | **Array** Array of field values {fieldId: 1, value: "my value"} | | orderRow\[\].quantity | **Number, mandatory**
Number of sold goods or services | | orderRow\[\].price | **Number, mandatory**
Net price | | orderRow\[\].listPrice | **Number, mandatory**
Gross price | | orderRow\[\].purchaseCost | **Number**
Purchase cost for the product | | orderRow\[\].product | **Object, mandatory**
Product you sold | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].productId | **Number**
Product ID for the product in the bundle | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].quantity | **Number**
Quantity for the product in the bundle | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].tierQuantity | **Number**
Tier quantity for the product in the bundle (this only applies for tiered products) | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].sortId | **Number**
Order this product appears in the UI in the bundle | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].price | **Number**
Net price for the product inside of the bundle | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].listPrice | **Number**
Gross price for the product inside of the bundle | | orderRow\[\].bundleRows\[\].purchaseCost | **Number**
Purchase cost for the product inside of the bundle | | value | **Number, read-only**
Total order sum | | weightedValue | **Number, read-only**
Total order sum \* with probability | | valueInMasterCurrency | **Number, read-only**
Total order sum in base currency | | weightedValueInMasterCurrency | **Number, read-only**
Total order sum in base currency \* with probability | | agreement | **Object, read-only**
Which agreement created this order | | userRemovable | **Bolean, read-only** User has right to delete Order in UI | | userEditable | **Bolean, read-only** User has right to edit Order in UI | | competitorId | **Number**
Find competitors you can choose between here: [https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/competitors/](https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/competitors/) | | lostReason | **Number**
Find lost Reason IDs here [https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/fieldTranslations/?type=orderlostreason](https://integration.upsales.com/api/v2/fieldTranslations/?type=orderlostreason) | | confirmedBudget | **read-only**
If sales rep confirmed budget with customer | | confirmedDate | **read-only**
If sales rep confirmed time frame with customer | | confirmedSolution | **read-only**
If sales rep confirmed with customer if our solution will solve their challenges | | salesCoach | **object, read-only**
Sales Coach feature inside Upsales | | locked | **depricated** | | notify | **boolean, write-only**, **default** **`true`**
If we should notify other users in the account about this order |

item.description:
Running your B2B events just got a whole lot easier. Upsales Events is now aligned with your Upsales CRM and marketing automation platform, adding new dimensions to your data. No more guesswork or communication breakdowns – instead you get the insight you need, before during and after events. Your messaging can be even more bespoke, segmented and at scale, with automated emails, landing pages, sharing tools, check-ins, guest list creation, and SMS functions. Having the platform connected with Events puts all the data in one place, helping to make things super-simple when it comes to nurturing attendees. ## Properties | Properties | Description | | ------------------------- |:---------------------------------------------------------------------:| | id |**Number, read-only**
Event ID | | name |**String, mandatory**
Event name | | description |**String**
Event description | | date |**Date, mandatory**
Event start date and time | | endDate |**Date**
Date and time when event is ended | | attendingScore |**Number**
Lead score for registering to the event | | checkInScore |**Number**
Lead score when you actually attained the event | | location |**String**
Event location | | venue |**String**
Event venue | | draft |**Number**
If event is a draft or not | | timezone |**string**
Which timezone the event will be in | | pageUrl |**string**
URL to landing page | | internalName |**string**
Internal name for this event | | isWebinar |**string**
If the event is a Webinar | | tickets |**number**
Maximum number of seats in this event | | waitingList |**string**
If the event allow waiting list | # Contacts Every contact will have a unique status in that event. ## Status - Added: Contact gets this status when they are added but not yet invited by email - Invited: Contact gets this status when they are invited by email to the event. - Attending: Contacts gets this status when they sign up for the event. - Attended: Contact gets this status when you check-in the contact. - Declined: Contact gets this status when a contact decline to attend the event. - Waiting List: Contact gets this status if the event is full and the contact waits to get a seat. - Error: Contact gets this status if the email has bounced or we found duplicated emails in the event

item.description:
Subscriptions are en entity which will automatically generate orders at a given interval. Most properties are therefore common between orders and subscriptions but there are additional metadata belonging to the subscription. ## Properties | Properties | Description | | --- | --- | | id | **Number, read-only**
Subscription ID | | description | **String, mandatory**
Subscription/Order description | | notes | **String**
Subscription notes | | user | **Object, mandatory**
User who did the subscription | | client | **Object, mandatory**
Company this subscription belongs to | | contact | **Object** Contact reference of the company that bought | | project | **Object** Campaign this subscription is added to | | regDate | **Date, read-only**
Subscription regdate | | stage | **Object, mandatory** Stage to which the orders from the subscription belongs to, must have 100 probability | | modDate | **Date, read-only** last modification date | | clientConnection | **Object** Second company connected to this order | | currencyRate | **Number** currency rate in relation to the base currency of the account | | currency | **String** Currency subscription and order are sold in | | custom | **Array** Array of field values {fieldId: 1, value: "my value"} | | orderRow | **Array, mandatory**
Order rows | | orderRow.id | **Number, read-only**
Id of orderRow | | orderRow.quantity | **Number, mandatory**
Number of sold goods or services | | orderRow.custom | **Array** Array of field values {fieldId: 1, value: "my value"} | | orderRow.sortId | **Number, mandatory**
Order of which order rows are presented in Upsales | | orderRow.price | **Number, mandatory**
Gross price (after discounts) | | orderRow.listPrice | **Number, mandatory**
net price (before discounts) | | orderRow.purchaseCost | **Number, mandatory**
Purchase cost of product, used to calculate contribution margin | | orderRow.product | **Object, mandatory**
Product you sold | | value | **Number, read-only**
Total order sum | | contributionMargin | **Number, read-only**
Total contribution margin | | purchaseCost | **Number, read-only**
Total purchase cost | | yearlyContributionMargin | **Number, read-only**
Total yearly contribution margin | | valueInMasterCurrency | **Number, read-only**
Total order sum in base currency | | yearlyValueInMasterCurrency | **Number, read-only**
Total yearly subscription sum in base currency | | yearlyValue | **Number, read-only**
Yearly subscription value | | metadata | **Object, mandatory**
Metadata about the subscription | | metadata.agreementDescription | **string, mandatory**
Should be the same as description | | metadata.agreementStartdate | **date, mandatory**
Date when the subscription started | | metadata.agreementEnddate | **date**
Date when the subscription stops, can be `null` if the subscription is ongoing | | metadata.agreementInvoiceStartdate | **date, create-only**
Date when the subscription was last renewed | | metadata.agreementRenewalDate | **date**
Date when the next period starts in the subscription, should be `null` if `periodLength` is 0 | | metadata.agreementRenewalActivityCreated | **date, read-only**
When the last reminder activity was created | | metadata.agreementNextOrderDate | **date, read-only**
When will the next order be created from the subscription | | metadata.agreementIntervalType | **number, mandatory**
Should always be 1 | | metadata.agreementIntervalPeriod | **number, mandatory**
How often new orders should be created from the subscription (1 monthly, 3 quarterly, 6 every six months, 12 every twelve month etc) | | metadata.agreementOrderCreationTime | **number, mandatory**
How many days before agreementRenewalDate to create the next order | | metadata.custom | **Array**
Array of field values {fieldId: 1, value: "my value"} | | metadata.orderSequenceNr | **number, read-only**
How many orders has been created from this subscription | | metadata.latestOrderId | **number, read-only**
Id of the last order created from this subscription | | metadata.agreementNotes | **string**
Notes on the subscription | | metadata.periodLength | **number, mandatory**
The length of each renewal period in months, if the subscription does not renew - then set this to 0 | | metadata.willCreateMoreOrders | **boolean, read-only**
If this subscription will create more orders | | userRemovable | **Bolean, read-only** User has right to delete Order in UI | | userEditable | **Bolean, read-only** User has right to edit Order in UI |
